mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in CPIAUCSL combined with unchanged or lower FEDFUNDS signals easing real-rate pressure and a lower discount rate on equities, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: disinflation phase with policy rate still restrictive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes of falling CPIAUCSL do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the sign of the return relation flips positive when CPIAUCSL is rising faster than FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months, labor-market deterioration signals an approaching earnings downgrade cycle and weaker risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-surge episodes are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 5d return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.25 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates expanding payroll income and retail demand, which supports near-term sales expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-slope state of PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to outperform baseline 20d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the signal and the joint interaction adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO above its recent trend reflects improving industrial capacity utilization and cyclical output momentum, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO trend states fail to generate above-median 60d SPX returns across walk-forward tests, or if the predictive sign turns negative during manufacturing expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST drops below its 6-month moving average after a prior rise in mortgage-sensitive housing activity, the market reads it as a demand slowdown and applies a weaker growth multiple, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend HOUST episodes do not precede negative 20d SPX returns more often than 55%, or if the average forward return after the trigger is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity multiples and tightens financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS regimes do not produce negative 60d SPX returns more often than the unconditional rate, or if the sign remains positive across two consecutive restrictive-policy windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates that output and nominal consumer spending are both improving, which raises forward revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive momentum in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to improve 60d SPX returns relative to either series alone, or if the combined state shows no outperformance in at least 3 consecutive expansion samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, a low UNRATE near 4% paired with stable PAYEMS indicates resilient labor demand without recessionary stress, which should keep S&P 500 returns positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment plus stable-payroll states do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than 55%, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value beyond UNRATE alone.
